document.title: Usability Engineering Plan For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering Plan A-UEP-001 Rev.5 for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Plans usability engineering, risk permission criteria, usability validation, accompanying documents, and training for the detector HAD1717MG,HAD1717MC.
keyPoints: The document is intended to plan the usability of the detector HAD1717MG,HAD1717MC.
Normative references include EN ISO 14971:2019 and EN 62366-1:2015.
HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.
The devices are not to be used for mammography.
Typical intended users are medical personnel with professional qualifications who are familiar with the User Manual and can understand Korean.

concepts.name: Risk control option analysis
concepts.evidence: There are three options for risk control options. To reduce risk using identified risk control methods, manufacturers should use one or more risk control options.

evidence.claim: The terms and definitions cite EN 62366:2008.
evidence.evidenceText: For the purposes of this standard, the terms and definitions given in "Common Criteria for Electrical and Mechanical Safety of Medical Devices" [Appendix 3] and EN 62366:2008 and the following definitions apply.

evidence.claim: The operator-equipment interface definition cites ANSI/AAMI HE 74:2011.
evidence.evidenceText: Means of communication between the operator and the ME equipment [ANSI/AAMI HE 74:2011, amend definition 3.24]

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are intended for general radiographic system digital imaging of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The patient population includes radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, chest, spinal, abdominal, hand, foot, and internal organs.
evidence.evidenceText: Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular, hand, foot and other internal organs.

evidence.claim: The product is not to be used for infant or pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Age: Available all people (Not to be used for infant or pregnant women)

evidence.claim: The intended user is a medical professional with a professional license.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: A medical professional with a professional license

evidence.claim: The intended user must be familiar with the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: - familiar with user manual

evidence.claim: The intended user's understandable language is Korean.
evidence.evidenceText: Language Understanding: Korean

evidence.claim: The operating condition limit is ambient temperature +5 to 40℃ and relative humidity 30 to 75 RH % without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:+5 ~ 40℃ - Relative humidity:30 ~ 75 RH % without condensation
evidence.pageTarget: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG Conditions of Use

evidence.claim: The storage condition limit is ambient temperature -10 to +50℃ and relative humidity 10 to 80 RH % without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:-10 ~ + 50℃ - Relative humidity:10 ~ 80 RH % without condensation
evidence.pageTarget: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG Conditions of Use

evidence.claim: The frequency of use section lists AC-DC Power Converter RPS-200-12 and an ESL of 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: part key: AC-DC Power Converter(RPS-200-12) Using a day: 8h ... ∴ ESL = 5 years
evidence.pageTarget: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG Conditions of Use

evidence.claim: The classification of applied parts is Not Applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.1.5 Classification of APPLIED PARTS Not Applied part

evidence.claim: Validation is performed to assess and reduce risk posed by correct use and usability related errors through the usability engineering process.
evidence.evidenceText: Validation is performed to assess and reduce the risk posed by correct use and usability related errors through the usability engineering process of the detector HAD1717MG (HAD17MC).

evidence.claim: The usability test staff size is 5 persons.
evidence.evidenceText: 7.1.3 Typical Intended Users (Test Staff) : 5 persons

evidence.claim: Risk control alternatives include inherent safety by design, preventive measures for medical devices or manufacturing processes, and information on stability.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Control Alternatives 1 inherent safety by design 2 Preventive measures for medical devices themselves or manufacturing processes 3 information on stability

evidence.claim: The risk acceptance zones are Green Zone, Widely Acceptable Zone Level 1 and Red Zone, Inacceptable Zone Level 2 to 16.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone, Widely Acceptable Zone: [Level 1] Red Zone, Inacceptable Zone: [Level 2~16]

evidence.claim: Primary motor functions for usability validation include connecting the power cord, cable code connection, main power button action, PC software settings, and x-ray irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: No. primary motor function ... 1 Connecting the power cord ... 2 Cable Code Connection ... 3 Main Power Button Action ... 4 PC Software Settings ... 5 x-ray irradiation

evidence.claim: Accompanying documents include Risk Management Plan A-RMP-5-01, Risk Management Report A-RMR-5-01, Usability Engineering Report A-UER-001, and TCF TCF-001.
evidence.evidenceText: Accompanying document - Risk Management Plan (A-RMP-5-01) - Risk Management Report(A-RMR-5-01) - Usability Engineering Report (A-UER-001) - TCF (TCF-001)

evidence.claim: If training on the specific medical device is required for safe and effective use of a primary operating function, the manufacturer shall provide or ensure availability of training materials or provide the training.
evidence.evidenceText: If training on the specific MEDICAL DEVICE is required for the safe and effective use of a PRIMARYOPERATING FUNCTION by the intended USER, the MANUFACTURER shall do at least one of the following: – provide the materials necessary for training – ensure that the materials necessary for training are available – provide the training.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text does not identify FDA or any authority/jurisdiction.
Authority or jurisdiction is not present in the source text.

The source contains apparent OCR or typographical issues, including 'primary motility', 'primary motor function', 'PRIMARYOPERATING', and 'HAD17MC' where the product model elsewhere appears as HAD1717MC.
The source text identifies document title, file number, revision number, revision date, product models, and applicable standards.
